text.title: Strategy Memo Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Strategy Memo Template application MUST enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Memo body MUST fit on two pages (~700 words). One page of vision, one page of three numbered bets + anti-bets + metrics.
rule.rationale: Brevity forces choice. A 40-slide deck hides indecision.

rule.statement: Pick EXACTLY three bets per cycle. Not two (under-commitment), not five (focus loss).
rule.rationale: Reforge memo discipline: three bets per horizon is the proven slot count for solo and small-team PMs.

rule.statement: Every bet has an explicit anti-bet ('we will NOT do X'). Anti-bets are first-class.
rule.rationale: Anti-bets prevent scope sprawl mid-quarter. The thing you refuse is part of the strategy.

rule.statement: Each bet carries one numeric success metric with target + deadline. No 'improve engagement'.
rule.rationale: Falsifiability is the bar: if the bet can't fail, it isn't a bet.

rule.statement: Memo carries a single named decision-owner (handle/email) who has signed off. No 'team agrees'.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership. Lenny/Reforge memos always name one accountable PM.
